programmes.show_description: British super group Cream captured live at the Royal Albert Hall in 1968. Tracks include 'Sunshine of Your Love', 'White Room', 'Politician' and many more.

programmes.show_name: A Lifetime Ago with The Baim Collection
programmes.show_description: A Printer's Tale. 1960. A 'fact full' film showing the work at Leeds printer John Waddington, featuring pictures printed for jigsaws, boxes for toothpaste and enormous posters.
1963. Who Killed Mr X? Directed by Don Weis. Stars Gene Barry, Gary Conway & Regis Toomey. A body turns up at a merry-go-round. There are no identifying papers on the body.
1965. Gig Young, David Niven and Charles Boyer are The Rogues, members of two related families of international forgers and conmen.
The Ordeal. 1955. Stars Richard Greene, Victor Woolf & Archie Duncan. Edgar's accused of murder. He is ordered to submit to trial by ordeal - plucking an iron bar from a boiling cauldron.
The Surgeon of Sangre Rojo. 1956. Stars Robert Shaw, Alec Mango & Dino Galvani. When a typhus outbreak hits New Providence, Captain Tempest launches a desperate mission to bring relief.
The Elixir. 1958. Stars: Conrad Phillips, Willoughby Goddard & Jennifer Jayne. A monastery is making a delicious brew, then donating the proceeds to founding a school for Swiss children.
Killer, Choose A Card. Directed by Aaron Spelling. Starring Don Durant. An old lady friend sends a desperate mail to Ringo claiming that the local townspeople are getting ready to lynch her.
1957. Crime. Stars Lionel Jeffries & Hazel Court. Reporter Joe (Jeff Morrow) finds his wife is the prime suspect in a murder investigation.
1958. Thriller. Directed by John Pomeroy. Stars William Sylvester & Marla Landi. A photographer investigates the murder of his friend, which occurred during a bank robbery for The Cause.
Dave Storm and The Diamonds perform the sensational hit 'If You Gotta Make a Fool of Somebody'.
1949. Crime Drama. Directed by Val Guest. Stars Garry Marsh, Jack Livesey & Jon Pertwee. Two London detectives order the repeat of a stage show after a spectator is shot during a performance.
1936. Drama. Directed by Bernard Vorhaus. Starring Margaret Rutherford and Anthony Bushell. Going 'straight' after being in jail, Jim is suspected when forged notes start circulating in the area.
1962. War. Director: Max Varnel. Stars Eric Flynn, Petra Davies, Francis De Wolff. When the Nazi's take over a small village during WWII, a local girl inadvertently falls in love with an officer.
1957. Western. Director: Allan Dwan. Stars Scott Brady, Anne Bancroft, Jay C. Flippen. A young lawyer travels to Mission, a small Texas border town, to even the score for the murder of his father.
1959. Murder mystery. Directed by Basil Dearden and stars Nigel Patrick, Yvonne Mitchell and Michael Craig. Superintendent Robert Hazard investigates a murder, which could be racially motivated.

programmes.show_name: The Lost Films of the Kursaal
programmes.show_description: A collection of films shot by the Kursaal Amusement Park owner Cliffton J. Morehouse providing a unique look at the amusement park in its heyday as seen through the eyes of its charismatic boss.
The Bargain. 1955. Stars Ron Randell, Joy Adamson & Bryan Forbes. Clifton enlists the help of his attractive wife to secure information from a business associate and is surprised by the offer.
A trip down memory lane takes Mike & Noel to 1930s Australia, fasten your seatbelts as we board a SuperJet to 1961 and the great Debbie Harry wants us to buy her jeans!
1989. Stars Diana Rigg, James Wilby & David McCallum. As Ruth's film is transmitted, Helena learns of a deception that is too tough to stomach. She sets a plan to take revenge on Ruth and George.
1956. Drama. Directed by Robert Aldrich. Stars Joan Crawford, Cliff Robertson & Vera Miles. A woman's romance with a younger man becomes a nightmare when his past comes back to haunt them both.
Directed by Francis A Searle. A 1969 comedy short centring around workmen digging a hole. Starring Arthur Lowe, Victor Maddern, Bill Maynard and Tim Barrett.
The Musgrave Ritual. Enjoy this classic short story read by Mike Read where Musgrave visits Holmes after the disappearance of his maid and the long time butler.
Fiddler on The Hoof - Part 1. Stars: Adam Faith, Iain Cuthbertson & Lynn Dalby. Two-part episode in which Budgie's domestic difficulties are complicated by his dad.

programmes.show_name: Voyage to the Planet of Prehistoric Women
programmes.show_description: 1968. Sci-fi. Director: Peter Bogdanovich. Stars Mamie Van Doren & Mary Marr. When a group of astronauts land on Venus they are surprised to find dangerous creatures and beautiful women.
2000. Crime. Directed by David Richards. Stars Ross Kemp, Maggie O'Neill & Julie Armstrong. A security guard becomes a national hero after fending off armed robbers. So why does he feel on edge?
1965. A Stitch In Crime. Classic crime series produced by Aaron Spelling and starring Anne Francis. Honey is hired to recover a stolen dress collection valued at $100,000.
An Adventure with Audubon. 1956. Stars Scott Forbes, Robert Cornthwaite & Barbara Eiler. Jim Bowie intervenes when his friend is mistaken for the missing son of Louis XVI and Marie Antoinette.
Founder of Talking Pictures TV and one half of the Footage Detectives, Noel Cronin, shares shows and films not to be missed throughout March.
1950. Comedy. Director: Godfrey Grayson. Stars Edward Rigby, Henry Mollison, Michael Ward. An Earl returns from a tropical island and finds a tribal king's daughter has stowed away in his luggage.
1954. Drama. Director Francis Searle and starring John Bentley, Kathleen Byron and Thea Gregory. The editor of a magazine is keen on the boss's daughter but her mother is keen on him.
1938. Drama. Director: Tim Whelan. Stars Charles Laughton, Vivien Leigh & Rex Harrison. A busker works a poor girl into his act. He leads her to fame even if it means sacrificing his love for her.
1952. Drama starring Leslie Dwyer and Lisa Daniely. Successful remake of one of the earliest British talkies. Set in the Hindle, two mill workers take off to Blackpool for the 'Wakes' holiday.
1959. Crime. Director Herbert Wilcox. Stars Anna Neagle, Anthony Quaylea and Zsa Zsa Gabor. A barrister tries to defend her client, a U.S. scientist for killing his bride, who was a U.S. spy.
1956. Comedy. Director: John Paddy Carstairs. Stars Norman Wisdom, Maureen Swanson & Jerry Desmonde. A window cleaner works at a country estate where the owner's son is threatened by kidnappers.
2004. Drama. Director: Coky Giedroyc. Stars Alun Armstrong, Geraldine McEwan & Jack Stanley. Carrie and her brother are evacuated to Wales where they meet friends who make their time delightful.
Barbed Wire. 1961. Stars Peter Graves, Anthony Wickert & Gerry Duggan. Cobb gets caught in a land war when he delivers barbed wire to some local farmers.
The Neptune Affair. 1964. Stars Robert Vaughn, David McCallum & Leo G. Carroll. Solo seeks to find a group of scientists who are trying to start World War 3.
Boy with Gun. Stars John Gregson, Alexander Davion and Daphne Anderson. A boy shoots a punk that threatened him with a knife and runs away, joined by a young escapee.
1947. Crime. Director: Roy Ward Baker. Stars: John Mills, Edward Chapman, Joan Greenwood. A man is suspected of murder, and due to a previous brain injury, he begins to doubt his own innocence.
Caroline Munro returns to the Cellar Club to delve into the exciting series The World of Hammer. This first episode explores the extensive career of Hammer star Peter Cushing.
Peter Cushing. 1994. Narrated by Oliver Reed. A look at the incredible, beloved actor Peter Cushing who, for two decades, brought dignity, passion and dramatic flair to his performances in film.
1932. Horror. Directors: Ernest B. Schoedsack & Irving Pichel. Stars Fay Wray, Joel McCrea, Leslie Banks & Robert Armstrong. Shipwrecked on an island, a hunter becomes the guest of Count Zaroff.
